enabled.description: Pointer to distinguish between not provided and false

author.description: Deprecated: Use authors or manufacturer instead.

repositoryProvider.description: either null or github or gitlab, etc.
either null or github or gitlab, etc.

lowRiskOpen.description: average age of non remediated (open) vulns

lowRiskRemediated.description: average time until vulns are remediated

csafPackageScope.description: only relevant for csaf references - NEEDS TO BE A VALID PURL

error.description: optional error message if the reference could not be processed

parentId.description: only set for projects, not for assets
if created as a child project

externalEntityId.description: only set if this is an external entity
only set if this is an external entity

x-enum-comments.RelationshipTypeAdvisory: flag relationships which stem from ingested security advisories
x-enum-comments.RelationshipTypeEUVD: flag relationships only imported through euvd explicitly

day.description: on the day 2024-08-12 the asset had a sumRisk of 25.

x-enum-comments.VulnStateFalsePositive: we can use that for crowdsource vulnerability management. 27 People marked this as false positive and they have the same dependency tree - propably you are not either
x-enum-comments.VulnStateFixed: we did not find the dependencyVuln anymore in the last scan!

version.description: either version or semver is defined

versionFixed.description: for non semver packages - if both are defined, THIS one should be used for displaying. We might fake semver versions just for database querying and ordering

versionIntroduced.description: for non semver packages - if both are defined, THIS one should be used for displaying. We might fake semver versions just for database querying and ordering

github_com_l3montree-dev_devguard_databa.description: Ensure foreign key field order matches Artifact primary key: ArtifactName, AssetVersionName, AssetID

day.description: on the day 2024-08-12 the asset had a sumRisk of 25.

repositoryId.description: the id will be prefixed with the provider name, e.g. github:<github app installation id>:123456

vulnAutoReopenAfterDays.description: Auto-reopen configuration - number of days after which closed/accepted vulnerabilities should be reopened

target_cve.description: the official CVE-XXXX-...  the external CVE-ID relates to

github_com_l3montree-dev_devguard_databa.description:
the provided sbom from cyclondx only contains the transitive dependencies, which do really get used
this means, that the dependency graph between people using the same library might differ, since they use it differently
we use edges, which provide the information, that a component is used by another component in one asset

id.description: ID might be a PURL - but not always. Sometimes it is a file path to a binary or a "fake node" we are adding during normalization

assetVersionName.description: Foreign key fields for AssetVersion relationship

componentPurl.description: will be ROOT for direct dependencies

email.description: Email is the email address of the user the invitation is for

organizationId.description: OrganizationID is the ID of the organization the invitation is for

opaqueId.description: only used by global policies maintained by the community and migrated by the system

organizationId.description: will be null for global policies

repositoryId.description: the id will be prefixed with the provider name, e.g. github:<github app installation id>:123456

AdminSignedAuth.description: Instance admin authentication using ECDSA P-256 HTTP message signing. Requires Signature, Signature-Input and Content-Digest headers.
AdminSignedAuth.name: Signature

BearerAuth.description: Bearer token authentication. Set value to: Bearer dvg_…
BearerAuth.name: Authorization

PATAuth.description: Personal Access Token authentication using HTTP request signing. Requires X-Signature and X-Fingerprint headers.
PATAuth.name: X-Signature

info.description: DevGuard Backend. Secure your Software Supply Chain. Attestation-based compliance as Code, manage your CVEs seamlessly, Integrate your Vulnerability Scanners, Security Framework Documentation made easy. OWASP Incubating Project
info.title: DevGuard API

post.description: Runs the full asset pipeline (scan, sync, risk calculation, statistics) for every asset on this instance. Returns an SSE stream with log, done, and error events. Subject to a 5-minute cooldown.

429.description: Cooldown not elapsed – try again later

post.summary: Trigger the asset pipeline for all assets

post.description: Runs the full asset pipeline for one asset identified by its UUID. Returns an SSE stream with log, done, and error events. Subject to a 5-minute cooldown per asset.

429.description: Cooldown not elapsed – try again later

post.summary: Trigger the asset pipeline for a single asset

post.description: Updates known fixed versions for tracked vulnerabilities. Returns an SSE stream with log, done, and error events. Subject to a 5-minute cooldown.

429.description: Cooldown not elapsed – try again later

post.description: Syncs open-source project metadata from deps.dev. Returns an SSE stream with log, done, and error events. Subject to a 5-minute cooldown (enforced across all API instances via the config DB).

429.description: Cooldown not elapsed – try again later

post.summary: Trigger the Open Source Insights daemon

post.description: Runs an incremental VulnDB import from upstream diffs. Sets the vulndb.vulndb config timestamp at the start of processing. Returns an SSE stream with log, done, and error events. Subject to a 5-minute cooldown.

429.description: Cooldown not elapsed – try again later

get.description: Returns the full tree of projects, sub-projects, assets, asset versions and artifacts that were dynamically created by an external inventory provider (e.g. k8s-devguard-image-inventory) for the given providerID.

post.description: Called by external inventory providers (e.g. k8s-devguard-image-inventory) to upsert or delete a dynamically managed project hierarchy. On verb=update, creates or updates the project, sub-project, asset, asset version and artifact, then processes the supplied CycloneDX SBOM and triggers a vulnerability scan. On verb=delete, removes the artifact/asset-version/asset/project entries cascading upward as long as no other data references them.
post.summary: Sync or delete an externally managed project/asset tree

get.summary: Get average remediation times for a release
Get risk history for a release

get.description: Returns aggregated security statistics for an organization, including vulnerability distribution, top vulnerable projects/assets/artifacts, most used components, common CVEs, risk history, remediation metrics, and ecosystem usage. All queries are executed in parallel.

parameters.description: Max number of top vulnerable projects/assets/artifacts to return (default: 5)
parameters.name: orgComponentsLimit

parameters.description: Max number of top CVEs to return (default: 5)
parameters.name: topCVEsLimit

parameters.description: Max number of top components to return (default: 5)
parameters.name: topComponentsLimit

parameters.description: Max number of top ecosystems to return (default: 5)
parameters.name: topEcosystemsLimit
